rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks into the target implementation scope when binding implementation-level behavior, not component type scope.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL component structure by only referencing ports, subcomponents, and connections explicitly defined in the input model.
Place contractual logic in the scope where the referenced elements are visible: type scope for interface features, implementation scope for subcomponent bindings.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `annex agree {** ... **};` for all AGREE contracts, and ensure all variables and constants are declared within the annex or derived from visible AADL features.
Only use numeric comparisons when the AADL model exposes real or integer ports—do not infer types or bounds.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL properties (e.g., Period, Deadline) as if they are AGREE variables.
Do not invent numeric constraints (e.g., x >= 0) unless explicitly stated in the requirement and supported by the AADL model.
irrelevant_topics: type scope range assumption and guarantee
AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables
